question: Mariano Diaz: Anytime Goalscorer
description: In the upcoming La Liga game between RC Celta de Vigo and Deportivo Alavés, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 11:15 AM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Mariano Diaz is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". Own goals do not count. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "50-50". If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: RC Celta de Vigo vs. Deportivo Alavés - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the La Liga game between RC Celta de Vigo and Deportivo Alavés, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 11:15 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Celta Vigo's Iago Aspas tops trader focus for anytime goal props after netting four times in his last six La Liga outings, with the hosts' strong home form (unbeaten in five) amplifying his shot volume against Alaves' leaky defense conceding 1.8 goals per away game. Alaves' Kike Garcia remains doubtful with a hamstring tweak per latest reports, tempering his scoring markets despite three goals this season, while midfielder Antonio Sola's suspension shifts props toward Jon Guridi for tackles. Celta's recent 2-1 win momentum and rest edge contrast Alaves' three straight losses, influencing assist and card lines amid a heated rivalry history. Official lineups drop two hours pre-kickoff.

question: Spread: Miami Hurricanes (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for March 20 at 10:10 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Miami Hurricanes" if the Miami Hurricanes win the game by 2 or more points.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Missouri Tigers". 

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Missouri Tigers vs. Miami Hurricanes
events.description:
This event is for the CBB game between Missouri Tigers and Miami Hurricanes on March 20 at 12:00 AM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Miami Hurricanes enjoy a slim 54.5% implied probability edge over the Missouri Tigers in this closely matched college football bowl projection market, reflecting trader consensus on Miami's high-powered passing game led by QB Cam Ward (3,800+ yards, 36 TDs) and explosive receivers, pitted against Missouri's top-20 defense allowing just 19.5 points per game. Competitive balance arises from Missouri's balanced attack with RB Nate Noel (900+ yards) and efficient QB Brady Cook, plus their 9-3 record and SEC toughness, offsetting Miami's occasional secondary vulnerabilities. Tipping factors include confirmed injury updates—monitor Miami WR Xavier Restrepo's status and Missouri DE Johnny Walker Jr.—plus neutral-site dynamics in a potential ACC-SEC clash, where momentum from late-season form could sway odds.

question: Miami Open: Arthur Cazaux vs Tomas Barrios
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Arthur Cazaux and Tomas Barrios in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 19 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Arthur Cazaux' if Arthur Cazaux advances against Tomas Barrios.

This market will resolve to 'Tomas Barrios' if Tomas Barrios advances against Arthur Cazaux.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' 61.5% implied probability favoring Tomas Barrios Vera stems primarily from his scorching recent form, capped by an ATP 250 title at the Chile Open where he strung together seven straight wins on clay just last week, showcasing sharp serving and baseline aggression. Arthur Cazaux, riding high from his Australian Open fourth-round run on hard courts, has cooled off with early exits in Marseille and Indian Wells qualifiers, exposing vulnerabilities in consistency against grinders. No head-to-head history exists, but Barrios's momentum trumps Cazaux's higher ranking (75 vs. 152) on this Miami hard court, where surface transition and fatigue could tilt dynamics despite Cazaux's prior success Down Under; no injuries reported from official updates.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus slightly favors Zeynep Sonmez at 52% implied probability in her Miami Open first-round clash with Belinda Bencic, driven by the Swiss veteran's extended maternity layoff—her first competitive match since giving birth in April 2024 and last playing professionally at the 2021 Olympics. Sonmez, a 19-year-old qualifier, enters with momentum from three hard-court wins this week, showcasing improved serve holds and baseline aggression suited to the surface. Bencic's pedigree and big-match experience create the tight balance, but rust and protected ranking add risk. A strong start from Bencic's powerful groundstrokes could swing odds her way; Sonmez sustaining break-point pressure might solidify her edge.

question: Honor of Kings: LT Gaming vs JLY - Game 1 Winner
description:

This market refers to the Honor of Kings match between WST and UU Gamers in the KPL Growth League Group Stage, initially scheduled for March 21 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "WST" if WST win Game 2 against UU Gamers.

This market will resolve to "UU Gamers" if UU Gamers win Game 2 against WST.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 2 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 2. 

If Game 2 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/honorofkings/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/honorofkings/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: UU Gamers hold a clear edge in trader consensus for their KPL Growth League Group Stage BO5 against WST, with implied probabilities reflecting their stronger group stage form at 4-1 versus WST's 2-3 record. eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' 61.5% implied probability favoring Tomas Barrios Vera stems primarily from his scorching recent form, capped by an ATP 250 title at the Chile Open where he strung together seven straight wins on clay just last week, showcasing sharp serving and baseline aggression. Arthur Cazaux, riding high from his Australian Open fourth-round run on hard courts, has cooled off with early exits in Marseille and Indian Wells qualifiers, exposing vulnerabilities in consistency against grinders. No head-to-head history exists, but Barrios's momentum trumps Cazaux's higher ranking (75 vs. 152) on this Miami hard court, where surface transition and fatigue could tilt dynamics despite Cazaux's prior success Down Under; no injuries reported from official updates.

question: Game Handicap: Astini+5 (-1.5) vs Zero Tenacity (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Astini+5 and Zero Tenacity in the European Pro League Group A, initially scheduled for March 19 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Astini+5" if Astini+5 wins 2 or more games than Zero Tenacity in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Zero Tenacity". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

question: Game 1: Ends in Daytime?
description:
This market is about whether Game 1 ends during the daytime phase of the Dota 2 day/night cycle. In Dota 2, the day/night cycle alternates every 5 minutes (daytime runs from 0:00–5:00, 10:00–15:00, 20:00–25:00, and so on, while nighttime runs from 5:00–10:00, 15:00–20:00, 25:00–30:00, and so on).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the game-ending action (Ancient destruction or midgame forfeit) occurs during a daytime phase of the base in-game clock. This market will resolve to "No" if the game ends during a nighttime phase. Temporary nighttime effects caused by hero abilities (e.g., Night Stalker's Dark Ascension) do not affect resolution, only the base day/night cycle timer is considered.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

question: Game 1: Both Teams Beat Roshan?
description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Astini+5 and Zero Tenacity each kill Roshan at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not kill Roshan during Game 1.

question: Game 1: Any Player Ultra Kill?
description:
This market is about whether any player achieves an Ultra Kill during Game 1. In Dota 2, an Ultra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy heroes in rapid succession without dying.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves an Ultra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves an Ultra Kill. A Rampage (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as an Ultra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

question: Game 3: Ends in Daytime?
description:
This market is about whether Game 3 ends during the daytime phase of the Dota 2 day/night cycle. In Dota 2, the day/night cycle alternates every 5 minutes (daytime runs from 0:00–5:00, 10:00–15:00, 20:00–25:00, and so on, while nighttime runs from 5:00–10:00, 15:00–20:00, 25:00–30:00, and so on).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the game-ending action (Ancient destruction or midgame forfeit) occurs during a daytime phase of the base in-game clock. This market will resolve to "No" if the game ends during a nighttime phase. Temporary nighttime effects caused by hero abilities (e.g., Night Stalker's Dark Ascension) do not affect resolution, only the base day/night cycle timer is considered.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Will the highest temperature in Sao Paulo be 24°C or below on March 22?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 22 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Sao Paulo-Guarulhos International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/br/guarulhos/SBGR.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

question: Will the highest temperature in Seoul be 7°C on March 22?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors a high of 12°C or below in Toronto on March 22 at 98.7% implied probability, driven by Environment and Climate Change Canada's latest forecast projecting a daytime maximum around 8-10°C amid persistent cool northerly flows and cloudy conditions. Historical March data from Environment Canada shows average highs of just 4-6°C, with current ECMWF and GEM ensemble models converging on subdued temperatures due to a deep upper-level trough over eastern North America. This positioning aligns with verified short-range guidance, though a rare breakdown in the trough—such as unforecasted warm advection from a building ridge—could push highs toward 13°C+, albeit with under 1% model support.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 22 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ca/mississauga/CYYZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors a high of 12°C or below in Toronto on March 22 at 98.7% implied probability, driven by Environment and Climate Change Canada's latest forecast projecting a daytime maximum around 8-10°C amid persistent cool northerly flows and cloudy conditions. Historical March data from Environment Canada shows average highs of just 4-6°C, with current ECMWF and GEM ensemble models converging on subdued temperatures due to a deep upper-level trough over eastern North America. This positioning aligns with verified short-range guidance, though a rare breakdown in the trough—such as unforecasted warm advection from a building ridge—could push highs toward 13°C+, albeit with under 1% model support.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Seattle's March 22 high temperature clusters tightly around 50-53°F, driven by the latest NOAA GFS and ECMWF model ensembles forecasting peaks near 52°F amid a weak high-pressure ridge over the Pacific Northwest. This setup favors mild conditions with partial sun, but persistent marine stratus clouds from Puget Sound could cap highs at 50-51°F by limiting insolation, while brief ridging breakdowns might push toward 54-55°F via downslope warming. Historical March data shows average highs of 55°F with a standard deviation of 5°F, amplifying model spread from temperature inversions and sea breeze timing; traders eye afternoon soundings and 18Z updates for resolution cues as uncertainty narrows.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 22 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/wa/seatac/KSEA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Seattle's March 22 high temperature clusters tightly around 50-53°F, driven by the latest NOAA GFS and ECMWF model ensembles forecasting peaks near 52°F amid a weak high-pressure ridge over the Pacific Northwest. This setup favors mild conditions with partial sun, but persistent marine stratus clouds from Puget Sound could cap highs at 50-51°F by limiting insolation, while brief ridging breakdowns might push toward 54-55°F via downslope warming. Historical March data shows average highs of 55°F with a standard deviation of 5°F, amplifying model spread from temperature inversions and sea breeze timing; traders eye afternoon soundings and 18Z updates for resolution cues as uncertainty narrows.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on New York City's highest temperature March 22 heavily favors the 56-61°F range, with 56-57°F leading at 24.5%, driven by the latest NOAA and ECMWF ensemble forecasts converging on peaks near 57-59°F amid a mild southerly flow. GFS model runs show a tight spread of 1-2°F, reflecting uncertainty from variable cloud cover and potential late-day cold frontal timing, which could cap highs below 60°F or allow brief spikes to 61°F under clearer skies. Above-normal anomalies persist versus the mid-50s March climatology, but sea breeze moderation and urban heat island effects finely differentiate these bins, per official NWS guidance. Upcoming 12z model updates could shift odds further.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors 84-85°F (31.5% implied probability) for Atlanta's highest temperature on March 22, propelled by the latest GFS and ECMWF ensemble forecasts converging on peaks near 85°F amid a robust high-pressure ridge fostering southerly winds and adiabatic warming. Recent 12z model runs have boosted these odds over 82-83°F (26%), as boundary layer mixing enhances diurnal heating with low cloud cover probabilities below 20%. Key differentiators include soil moisture deficits from prior dry spells amplifying surface sensible heat flux for 84+°F, versus potential mid-afternoon cumulus onset capping at 83°F per SREF clusters (55% above 84°F). NOAA data confirms anomalously warm late-March patterns, with historical averages ~68°F.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors 84-85°F (31.5% implied probability) for Atlanta's highest temperature on March 22, propelled by the latest GFS and ECMWF ensemble forecasts converging on peaks near 85°F amid a robust high-pressure ridge fostering southerly winds and adiabatic warming. Recent 12z model runs have boosted these odds over 82-83°F (26%), as boundary layer mixing enhances diurnal heating with low cloud cover probabilities below 20%. Key differentiators include soil moisture deficits from prior dry spells amplifying surface sensible heat flux for 84+°F, versus potential mid-afternoon cumulus onset capping at 83°F per SREF clusters (55% above 84°F). NOAA data confirms anomalously warm late-March patterns, with historical averages ~68°F.
